title: Claude helper model-tier defaults and escalation policy
description: Implement the operator directive that Claude-created helper agents default to auto permission mode plus Haiku model tier, and require a written reviewed escalation request for higher Claude model tiers. Document a practical tier-capability rubric so reviewers choose the right model level generously when the reasoning is sound, without forcing low-tier or high-tier use by default.
acceptance_criteria: Claude helper spawn defaults are documented as auto permission mode plus Haiku model tier, with visible wezterm tabs still mandatory.
Higher Claude helper tiers require a written request with reasoning and review by another core agent before use.
A tier capability rubric explains when Haiku is enough and when Sonnet or Opus is justified, including the principle that sound reasoning should be approved generously.
The hcom Claude default args are updated to include auto permission mode and Haiku.
